# Questions in Livestream 108

Live Stream #108: The Many Views of Heck

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/y5KgOW9yQ3w?t=717" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">11:57</a> | Could there be surprising people in heaven? | He said entry into heaven is fundamentally about relation to Christ rather than passing a theology exam, and he left some room for a `second chance` idea for those who never heard the gospel. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/y5KgOW9yQ3w?t=1412" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">23:32</a> | How can we tell an Egyptian loanword from a shared Afroasiatic inheritance? | He said the best clue is distribution: if a word has deep history in one branch and later enters another, it is likely a loan. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/y5KgOW9yQ3w?t=1593" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">26:33</a> | What was he thinking with the stream title jokes, the missing TV, and the Mount Ebal tablet translation? | He treated the first two as jokes, then criticized the Mount Ebal epigraphy as pareidolia and said the team should have reported an honest negative result. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/y5KgOW9yQ3w?t=2210" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">36:50</a> | What if an old pharaoh could not finish the Sed festival? | He said there is no record of a pharaoh failing it, no time limit was attached, and physical or mental incapacity could lead to proxy rule or a `Renaissance` arrangement. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/y5KgOW9yQ3w?t=2667" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">44:27</a> | How would he respond to the claim that Exodus 21:2-6, especially verse 4, is immoral? | He said the law regulates an already immoral cultural situation and tries to protect vulnerable women within that setting rather than endorsing the culture itself. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/y5KgOW9yQ3w?t=3427" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">57:07</a> | Why does God seem to sanction revenge killing Saul's descendants in 2 Samuel 21? | He argued that Saul broke a treaty ratified before God, likely triggering covenant sanctions, so the famine and deaths relate to repairing that broken covenant. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/y5KgOW9yQ3w?t=3938" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:05:38</a> | What archaeological discovery would be hardest to reconcile with the biblical narrative? | He said the Mount Ebal amulet would be a major problem if the claims were true, but he does not think the claimed reading and dating are legitimate. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/y5KgOW9yQ3w?t=4109" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:08:29</a> | Which would be the bigger problem, the Mount Ebal amulet or the Jerusalem curse tablet? | He said the amulet would be bigger if its reading and dating were correct; a Jerusalem curse tablet would fit an Amorite city around 1400 BC, though he rejected both epigraphies. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/y5KgOW9yQ3w?t=4189" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:09:49</a> | What are some strong popular-level books on Old Testament reliability? | He recommended titles such as James Hoffmeier on archaeology, Kenneth Kitchen on Old Testament reliability, Peter J. Williams on the gospels, and work by John Walton for context. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/y5KgOW9yQ3w?t=4383" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:13:03</a> | Is it true that scholars once believed ancient Israel was a myth? | He said some Enlightenment-era skeptics did, especially following Voltaire, but archaeology has forced many such claims about Israel, Hittites, Belshazzar, and others to be walked back. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/y5KgOW9yQ3w?t=4898" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:21:38</a> | Was the first catapult in the world invented in Judah? | He said it is possible but uncertain because the relevant biblical device may only have dropped stones rather than used stored mechanical energy like a true catapult. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/y5KgOW9yQ3w?t=5074" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:24:34</a> | What is the Late Bronze Age context of Genesis 6:4 compared with a Second Temple Judaism context? | He contrasted Heiser's Second Temple reading with a Bronze Age priestly-vow context, taking Nephilim as offspring connected to broken vows rather than demigod beings. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/y5KgOW9yQ3w?t=5545" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:32:25</a> | How plausible is it to deny the historicity of Jesus, Paul, or even early Christians altogether? | He said the apostolic fathers and their teacher-student chain make that skepticism very hard to sustain. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/y5KgOW9yQ3w?t=5683" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:34:43</a> | Does anyone doubt Socrates lived? | He said no, even though the evidence mainly comes through Plato and Xenophon, and contrasted that with skeptical claims about Christian figures. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/y5KgOW9yQ3w?t=5943" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:39:03</a> | Did ancient Egyptian women have equal rights to men of the same social class? | He said Egyptian women had unusually broad rights for the ancient Near East, including divorce, property, adoption, and economic options, with exceptions by status. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/y5KgOW9yQ3w?t=6407" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:46:47</a> | Are there good books on disturbing Old Testament passages besides Paul Copan? | He said Copan fills an important niche, older broad works such as Gleason Archer's and the McDowells' attempts have problems, and slavery-law literature is more specialized. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/y5KgOW9yQ3w?t=7074" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:57:54</a> | What about the claim that Old Testament sacrifices purified holy spaces while Jesus forgives sins, and that New Testament authors confused this? | He called it partly true but partly mistaken: purification sacrifices and sin forgiveness are distinct categories, yet Jesus' sacrifice also covers the broader sacrificial system. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/y5KgOW9yQ3w?t=7495" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:04:55</a> | Did Tertullian believe the Spirit and Son were created first but temporary and would one day cease? | He said he would need the exact source and context because Tertullian is often misquoted, especially when he is quoting and then refuting modalist opponents. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/y5KgOW9yQ3w?t=7892" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:11:32</a> | What are his thoughts on Total War: Pharaoh? | He had not seen or played it yet, but said the described setting sounded like late Dynasty 19 or early Dynasty 20 and welcomed more Egypt-themed games. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/y5KgOW9yQ3w?t=8256" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:17:36</a> | What can be used instead of Strong's Concordance? | He recommended HALOT for Hebrew and Aramaic, BDAG for New Testament Greek, and Holladay's concise Hebrew-Aramaic lexicon as a cheaper Old Testament option. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/y5KgOW9yQ3w?t=8790" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:26:30</a> | Who is the naked young man in Mark 14:51-52? | He said the text does not identify him and leaned toward a literary Gestalt for the fleeing disciples, while allowing that he could have been a real person. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/y5KgOW9yQ3w?t=9160" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:32:40</a> | What is his view of Michael Heiser's argument that the tribes under the conquest ban were Nephilim tribes? | He said the spy report is exaggerated, Heiser conflates Nephilim and Rephaim, and the biblical text does not require that identification even if Second Temple literature supports it. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/y5KgOW9yQ3w?t=10157" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:49:17</a> | How does Christianity influence David and Kiara's lives? | He said it led him to give up a high-paying career, pursue ministry and education work, and see God's provision through difficult turns. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/y5KgOW9yQ3w?t=10730" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:58:50</a> | What are his thoughts on Builders of Egypt? | He said he did not know whether it was a game or video series and could not comment because he had not seen or played it. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/y5KgOW9yQ3w?t=10804" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:00:04</a> | What did he think of Rob's Mount Ebal short? | He said it was well done and funny, but the image of Scott Stripling dancing was burned into his memory and he wished he had not seen it. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/y5KgOW9yQ3w?t=10923" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:02:03</a> | Does Heiser's view provide a strong apologetic for conquest allegations compared with human-Nephilim views? | He said no; even if Heiser were right, it would not solve the genocide objection, and genre and lexical issues provide better explanations. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/y5KgOW9yQ3w?t=11381" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:09:41</a> | What were the most distant regions or peoples ancient Egyptians had contact with in Europe, Asia, and Africa? | He said Egyptian trade contacts changed by period but reached Punt/Ethiopia, Cyprus and the Greek islands, Anatolia, Spain's Atlantic coast, India, and by Dynasty 20 possibly China. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/y5KgOW9yQ3w?t=11777" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:16:17</a> | Is the idea that demons are spirits of dead Nephilim unbiblical? | He said it is not directly contradicted but is unsupported or non-biblical, and the evidence leans against it. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/y5KgOW9yQ3w?t=12030" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:20:30</a> | Could Jude and Enoch be quoting a shared tradition rather than Jude quoting Enoch, and could the prophecy be separate from the rest of Enoch? | He said either Jude quoting Enoch or both drawing from a shared tradition is possible, but the question remains speculative and hard to prove. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/y5KgOW9yQ3w?t=12264" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:24:24</a> | Are there relations between Israelite timekeeping and the calendars of Canaanites or Egyptians? | He said Israel's calendar began as lunar, adopted features from neighboring systems over time, used regnal dating under Egyptian influence, and later absorbed Mesopotamian intercalation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/y5KgOW9yQ3w?t=12683" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:31:23</a> | Could Goliath have suffered from the same pituitary-gland problem as Robert Wadlow? | He doubted it because Goliath and related giants are portrayed as accomplished warriors, unlike most modern pituitary-gigantism cases, but said skeletons would be needed to know. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/y5KgOW9yQ3w?t=12896" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:34:56</a> | Does 2 John still apply if heretical missionaries such as Mormons or Jehovah's Witnesses come to a home? | He said the passage concerns giving false teachers a platform in a house church, not ordinary hospitality or evangelistic conversation, so the application depends on whether they are being allowed to teach. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/y5KgOW9yQ3w?t=13415" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:43:35</a> | Should Christians still celebrate the Sabbath? | He said Christians are not under the Sinai covenant requirement, but taking a Sabbath rest can still be healthy and beneficial. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/y5KgOW9yQ3w?t=13622" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:47:02</a> | How are ancient dates dated when dates can be unreliable, such as for the late Exodus period? | He said chronologists use king lists and synchronisms such as battles, letters, marriages, and royal gifts to align rulers and rule out impossible date schemes. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/y5KgOW9yQ3w?t=14144" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:55:44</a> | How serious was the insubordination of the Egyptian midwives in Exodus 1? | He said Pharaoh's order violated Egyptian justice traditions, so their refusal was probably not treated as severe, though they likely still faced brutal questioning or beating. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/y5KgOW9yQ3w?t=14635" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:03:55</a> | In Egyptian naming magic, what was a person's real name, and did Egyptians understand the arbitrariness of names? | He said Egyptians distinguished a public name from a hidden true name that functioned like a magical key to control a being. |
